description:
You are a monitoring specialist who has just logged in to a brand-new Linux host
    (home directory: /home/user).  
    Management has asked you to set up a very small “poor-man’s alerting” prototype
    so they can understand what memory-related alerts will look like.

    Goal
    1. Create a workspace called /home/user/monitoring with two sub-directories:
       a. /home/user/monitoring/config  
       b. /home/user/monitoring/logs
    2. Write a plain-text configuration file
       /home/user/monitoring/config/threshold.conf  
       containing a single variable assignment (no spaces around the “=” sign):
            THRESHOLD=75
       …and nothing else (one trailing newline is fine).
    3. Implement an executable Bash script
       /home/user/monitoring/check_memory.sh  
       that behaves as follows:
       • It reads THRESHOLD from   /home/user/monitoring/config/threshold.conf  
         (fail if the file/variable is missing).  
       • NORMAL MODE (called without arguments):  
         – obtain total and used memory in MB from the command  `free -m`.  
         – compute percentage_used = (used * 100 / total), rounded to the nearest
           integer (you may use integer arithmetic).  
         – If percentage_used is greater than THRESHOLD, append a single-line alert
           to   /home/user/monitoring/logs/memory_alert.log   using the exact format

             [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] ALERT: Memory usage is <PERC>% (Used: <USED>MB / Total: <TOTAL>MB)

           where the timestamp is the local time produced by the command
           `date "+%F %T"`; <PERC>, <USED>, and <TOTAL> are the computed integers.
       • TEST MODE (called with the flag `--test`):  
         – ignore the real system values and hard-code  
           TOTAL=1000, USED=900, PERC=90  
           (this ensures the alert will fire).  
         – append exactly one line, verbatim:

             [TEST] [ALERT] Memory usage is 90% (Used: 900MB / Total: 1000MB)

       • The script must be executable by the current user.

    4. Make sure the following are true when you finish (automated tests rely on it):
       • File /home/user/monitoring/config/threshold.conf exists with the exact
         single line “THRESHOLD=75”.  
       • File /home/user/monitoring/check_memory.sh exists, is executable
         (owner execute bit on), and is a valid Bash script (shebang is “#!/bin/bash” or
         “#!/usr/bin/env bash”).  
       • Directory /home/user/monitoring/logs exists.  
       • After you have run the script once in TEST MODE, the file
         /home/user/monitoring/logs/memory_alert.log exists and contains at least
         one line identical to:

             [TEST] [ALERT] Memory usage is 90% (Used: 900MB / Total: 1000MB)

    Constraints / helpful notes
    • Everything must be done as the unprivileged user “user”; no sudo needed.  
    • Only standard GNU utilities available on a minimal Debian/Ubuntu image may be
      assumed.  
    • The whole solution should realistically require around 5–10 shell commands
      once you know what to type (mkdir, echo/cat, chmod, etc.).  
    • Feel free to test your script in normal mode, but the automated grader will
      only check the presence of the exact TEST MODE line above.

    When you are finished, the paths and files named above must exist exactly as
    specified so the automated tests can locate and verify them.
    (No extra sub-directories, different names, or additional content outside the
    described files.)
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data used by the grader; do NOT reveal in public description -->

    <!-- Files/directories that MUST exist at the end -->
    <requiredPaths>
        <dir>/home/user/monitoring</dir>
        <dir>/home/user/monitoring/config</dir>
        <dir>/home/user/monitoring/logs</dir>
        <file>/home/user/monitoring/config/threshold.conf</file>
        <file>/home/user/monitoring/check_memory.sh</file>
        <file>/home/user/monitoring/logs/memory_alert.log</file>
    </requiredPaths>

    <!-- Expected file contents -->
    <fileContent path="/home/user/monitoring/config/threshold.conf">
THRESHOLD=75
    </fileContent>

    <!-- The log file must have at least the following exact line -->
    <logMustContain path="/home/user/monitoring/logs/memory_alert.log">
[TEST] [ALERT] Memory usage is 90% (Used: 900MB / Total: 1000MB)
    </logMustContain>

    <!-- Executable requirement -->
    <executable path="/home/user/monitoring/check_memory.sh">true</executable>
